parameters.name: GitHub PAT
parameters.description: Fine-grained PAT with 'Administration: Read and write' on the repository. The runner uses it once at boot to fetch a registration token; not stored on GitHub.

parameters.name: Domain
parameters.description: Fully qualified domain name for the certificate

parameters.name: Email
parameters.description: Email address for Let's Encrypt registration and expiry notifications.

parameters.name: CA Certificate
parameters.description: Install CA chain certificate (chain.pem).

parameters.name: SAN (Subject Alternative Names)
parameters.description: Domain(s) for the certificate. Comma-separated for multi-domain SAN (e.g. app.example.com or auth.example.com,api.example.com). Supports wildcards (*.example.com).

parameters.name: Staging Mode
parameters.description: Use Let's Encrypt staging server (for testing, no rate limits).

parameters.name: Certificate Directory
parameters.description: Container directory where certificates are written

parameters.name: Needs CA Certificate
parameters.description: Whether to write the CA/intermediate certificate (chain.pem)

parameters.name: Needs Server Certificate
parameters.description: Whether to issue a server certificate (privkey, cert, fullchain)

parameters.name: Samba Configuration File
parameters.description: Base64 encoded content of the addon configuration file

parameters.name: Addon Packages
parameters.description: Additional packages from addons (merged with base packages).

parameters.name: Addon File Path
parameters.description: Target path in format {volume_key}:{filename}. Example: samba_config:smb.conf

parameters.name: Addon Volumes
parameters.description: Additional volumes from addons (merged with base volumes).

parameters.name: All Stack IDs
parameters.description: Auto-injected by backend: JSON array of all selected stack IDs, written as separate :stack-id markers

parameters.name: Alpine Mirror
parameters.description: Alpine Linux APK mirror URL (e.g., 'http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine'). If empty, default repositories will be used. Should include base URL without version/repository path.

parameters.name: API Login Password
parameters.description: Password for shared api_login role (PostgREST). Resolved from .env if available.

parameters.name: Application Dependencies
parameters.description: Auto-injected by backend: JSON array of application dependencies

parameters.name: Application Name
parameters.description: Name of the application (used for schema/role naming: <app>_data, <app>_app, etc.)

parameters.name: App User Password
parameters.description: Password for the <app>_app database user

parameters.name: Application ID
parameters.description: Application identifier for matching the Docker image

parameters.name: Application Name
parameters.description: Human-readable application name

parameters.name: Architecture
parameters.description: Optional: CPU architecture for pct create (amd64, arm64, armhf, i386). Only used for OCI images. For regular LXC templates, Proxmox auto-detects the architecture.

parameters.name: Audio Card
parameters.description: Audio card identifier in format card0, card1, etc. (e.g., card0 for first audio card)

parameters.name: Bind Privileged Port
parameters.description: Allow service to bind to privileged ports (e.g., 80, 443) by setting CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE capability

parameters.name: Bootstrap Zitadel
parameters.description: Set to 'true' to run Zitadel bootstrap (creates project, service account, invalidates admin PAT)

parameters.name: Network bridge
parameters.description: Bridge to attach (e.g. vmbr0).

parameters.name: CA Cert
parameters.description: Base64-encoded CA public certificate PEM

parameters.name: Cert Dir Override
parameters.description: If set, use this path directly as cert directory instead of computing from shared_volpath

parameters.name: Cloudflare API Token
parameters.description: Cloudflare API token with Zone:DNS:Edit permission on the mail domain zone. Provided via the cloudflare stack. Required when smtp_own_domain is true.

parameters.name: File Paths to Check
parameters.description: Newline-separated list of file paths to verify inside the container.

parameters.name: Check OIDC API Protected
parameters.description: Set to true to verify the deployer API requires authentication.

parameters.name: Check OIDC Enabled
parameters.description: Set to true to verify OIDC is enabled on this deployer instance.

parameters.name: Check OIDC Machine Login
parameters.description: Set to true to verify end-to-end OIDC machine login.

parameters.name: TLS Port to Check
parameters.description: Port number to verify TLS connection on.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command to run (also used as service and user name)

parameters.name: Command Line Arguments
parameters.description: Arguments for the command. Available variables: $HOME_DIR, $DATA_DIR, $SECURE_DIR

parameters.name: Docker Compose File
parameters.description: Docker Compose YAML file (upload). Upload this file first to extract service information.

parameters.name: Env Key in docker-compose.yml
parameters.description: The environment key to set (e.g. CF_API_TOKEN).

parameters.name: Compose Project
parameters.description: Project name for Docker Compose (used for named volumes and directory). Defaults to hostname if not provided.

parameters.name: Container Device Path
parameters.description: Device path inside the container

parameters.name: Container Device Paths
parameters.description: Optional: Comma-separated container device paths (default: auto-detect based on card number). Note: This parameter is currently not used, devices are mapped to standard /dev/snd/* paths.

parameters.name: Target Database
parameters.description: Database to create schemas in (default: postgres)

parameters.name: Database Name
parameters.description: Name of the PostgreSQL database to create

parameters.name: Debian Mirror
parameters.description: Debian/Ubuntu APT mirror URL (e.g., 'http://deb.debian.org/debian'). If empty, default repositories will be used.

parameters.name: Zitadel VM ID
parameters.description: VM ID of the Zitadel dependency container.

parameters.name: Deployer Base URL
parameters.description: Auto-injected by backend: External deployer URL (e.g. https://deployer.example.com). Used for redirect when set.

parameters.name: Disk size
parameters.description: Rootfs size for the container in GiB. Apps that bundle a large image (Node.js, JDK, full Debian base) should override this in their application.json. Default fits Alpine + small OCI images (~200 MiB used).

parameters.name: Docker Hub Username
parameters.description: Docker Hub username for authenticated pulls (200 pulls/6h instead of 100). Leave empty to run anonymously.

parameters.name: Domain Suffix
parameters.description: Global DNS domain suffix — provided by 150-conf-setup-oidc-client.

parameters.name: Environment File (.env)
parameters.description: Environment variables file (.env) template (upload, optional). If it contains {{ markers }}, users must upload a real .env at deployment time.

parameters.name: Environment Variables
parameters.description:
Environment variables in key=value format, one per line. Each line sets an environment variable in the container configuration. Example:
MYAPP_HOST=localhost
MYAPP_PORT=8080
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost:5432/mydb

parameters.name: Expected Versions
parameters.description: Comma-separated service=version pairs (e.g. zitadel=v4.13.1,traefik=v3.6.13)

parameters.name: Credential Password
parameters.description: Password for credential nodes (e.g. database password)

parameters.name: Flow Source
parameters.description: URL or path to the flow JSON file to import

parameters.name: Credential Username
parameters.description: Username for credential nodes (e.g. database user)

parameters.name: GID
parameters.description: Optional: GID of the group in the container who should have access to the input device (LXC only). Default is 0 (root). For VMs, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: Gitea Admin Password
parameters.description: Admin password for Gitea REST API access (from gitea stack)

parameters.name: Group Name
parameters.description: Optional group name (will be created if not exists)

parameters.name: USB Serial Port
parameters.description: USB serial port device path on the host

parameters.name: Hostname
parameters.description: Container hostname. Used as compose_project default when none was given. Auto-resolved from container config on reconfigure.

parameters.name: HTTP Port
parameters.description: Port number for PostgREST HTTP server (default: 3000)

parameters.name: HTTPS Port
parameters.description: The HTTPS port to listen on (default: 1443, rootless containers cannot bind 443).

parameters.name: Icon Base64
parameters.description: Auto-injected by backend: Base64-encoded application icon

parameters.name: Icon MIME Type
parameters.description: Auto-injected by backend: Icon MIME type (image/svg+xml or image/png)

parameters.name: Initial Command
parameters.description: Initial command to execute in the container (lxc.init_cmd). Useful for OCI images that require a specific start command.

parameters.name: Live Replug (Host Installation)
parameters.description: Install a watcher service on the host to handle device replug events

parameters.name: Is Deployer
parameters.description: Auto-injected by backend: Set to 'true' when installing proxvex itself

parameters.name: Runner Labels
parameters.description: Comma-separated labels used in workflow `runs-on:` selectors.

parameters.name: Environment Variable Name
parameters.description: Name of the env variable (e.g. POSTGRES_PASSWORD). Becomes lxc.environment.<NAME>=<VAL>.

parameters.name: Mapped GID
parameters.description: Optional: The mapped GID on the host (output from setup-lxc-uid-mapping.py in template 102). If not provided, the script will calculate it. Use this when using 1:1 UID mapping instead of the standard offset.

parameters.name: Mapped UID
parameters.description: Optional: The mapped UID on the host (output from setup-lxc-uid-mapping.py in template 102). If not provided, the script will calculate it. Use this when using 1:1 UID mapping instead of the standard offset.

parameters.name: Memory in MB
parameters.description: Memory for the container in MB

parameters.name: nameserver4
parameters.description: IPv4 DNS nameserver (e.g. 192.168.1.1). Optional.

parameters.name: nameserver6
parameters.description: IPv6 DNS nameserver (e.g. fd00:...::1 or 2001:...::1). Optional.

parameters.name: New Value
parameters.description: New value for the shell variable.

parameters.name: OCI Image
parameters.description: Optional: OCI image reference used to create the container (e.g. docker://mariadb:11.0.2). If provided, it will be written into the Proxmox notes (description) as an identifiable line.

parameters.name: Version
parameters.description: OCI image tag / version to pull (e.g. '0.5.28', 'v4.12.3'). Explicit versions keep registry-mirror caches deterministic and make tests reproducible. Apps typically template this into their `oci_image` property (e.g. `ghcr.io/foo/bar:{{oci_image_tag}}`). Reconfigure does not execute this template, so the reconfigure path inherits the tag already stored in the container's notes.

parameters.name: OIDC Application Name
parameters.description: Name of the OIDC application in Zitadel (defaults to hostname). Used when the deployer can talk to the Zitadel Management API to auto-create or look up the client.

parameters.name: Callback Path
parameters.description: Path the OIDC provider redirects to after login (from app.properties).

parameters.name: OIDC Client ID
parameters.description: Existing OIDC client ID from Zitadel. Provide together with Client Secret and Issuer URL when auto-provisioning via the Zitadel Management API is not available (e.g. deployer OIDC login is disabled or pointing at an external Zitadel).

parameters.name: OIDC Client Secret
parameters.description: OIDC client secret for client_credentials fallback (provided by addon-oidc).

parameters.name: OIDC Issuer URL
parameters.description: OIDC issuer URL (e.g. https://zitadel.example.com). Required together with Client ID and Secret for the manual entry mode.

parameters.name: Zitadel Project Name
parameters.description: Name of the Zitadel project for this application (defaults to hostname). Used for auto-provisioning via the Zitadel Management API.

parameters.name: OIDC Required Role
parameters.description: Zitadel project role required for access (from app.properties). If empty, any authenticated user is allowed.

parameters.name: OIDC Roles
parameters.description: JSON array of role definitions from application.json (injected by backend)

parameters.name: OS Type
parameters.description: Operating system type: 'alpine' for Alpine Linux (apk) or 'debian'/'ubuntu' for Debian/Ubuntu (apt).

parameters.name: Owned Paths
parameters.description: Space-separated list of files/directories the user should own with rw access

parameters.name: Package name
parameters.description: Name of the npm package to install

parameters.name: URL of the public key for verifying the package
parameters.description: URL of the public key for verifying the package

parameters.name: Packages
parameters.description: Space-separated list of packages to install. For Alpine Linux, use APK package names. For Debian/Ubuntu, use apt package names.

parameters.name: URL of the APK package
parameters.description: URL of the APK package to be installed

parameters.name: Platform
parameters.description: Target platform (e.g., linux/amd64, linux/arm64). Auto-detects if not specified.

parameters.name: Previous VM ID
parameters.description: Previous container VMID (upgrade/reconfigure). Used to prefer reusing that CT's existing volumes over any other historical match.

parameters.name: Registry Password
parameters.description: Password for registry authentication (optional, required for private images)

parameters.name: Registry Token
parameters.description: Bearer token for registry authentication (optional, alternative to username/password)

parameters.name: Registry Username
parameters.description: Username for registry authentication (optional, required for private images)

parameters.name: GitHub Repository URL
parameters.description: Full URL of the GitHub repository the runner registers against (e.g. https://github.com/proxvex/proxvex).

parameters.name: REST Content-Type
parameters.description: Content-Type header sent with the POST.

parameters.name: Health Endpoint Path
parameters.description: Path polled until the application reports readiness.

parameters.name: REST Endpoint Path
parameters.description: Path of the local-settings import endpoint.

parameters.name: REST Payload (base64)
parameters.description: Base64-encoded ZIP payload. Defaults to the uploaded ZIP content.

parameters.name: REST Base URL
parameters.description: Base URL of the application REST API (relative to the container).

parameters.name: Rootfs Storage
parameters.description: Proxmox storage for the container rootfs

parameters.name: Runner Name
parameters.description: Display name shown in GitHub Settings → Actions → Runners.

parameters.name: Script File
parameters.description: Filename under ${VOLUME_DIR}/on_start.d/ (e.g. acme-renew.sh).

parameters.name: Shell Variable Name
parameters.description: Shell variable whose value is patched (e.g. CF_API_TOKEN).

parameters.name: DNS Search Domain
parameters.description: DNS search domain for the container's resolv.conf. Default (empty) avoids inheriting the PVE host's hostname domain (e.g. 'cluster' when the host is 'pve1.cluster'), which can break bare-hostname DNS resolution from inside docker containers (Go's resolver and dnsmasq embedded DNS append the search suffix and don't fall back to the bare name). Set explicitly only if you need a specific search domain.

parameters.name: Server Cert
parameters.description: Base64-encoded server certificate PEM (signed by backend)

parameters.name: Server Key
parameters.description: Base64-encoded server private key PEM (signed by backend)

parameters.name: Shared Volume Path
parameters.description: Deployer-global shared volume path on the PVE host — used by app scripts that edit files relative to the LXC volume (e.g. Node-RED settings.js).

parameters.name: Samba Password
parameters.description: Password for Samba authentication

parameters.name: Samba Username
parameters.description: Username for Samba authentication

parameters.name: SMTP Host
parameters.description: SMTP server hostname (e.g. smtp.mailbox.org). Leave empty to skip SMTP setup.

parameters.name: Mail domain
parameters.description: Apex domain the mail is sent from (e.g. example.com). Looked up in Cloudflare and used as the zone for MX/SPF records.

parameters.name: MX target
parameters.description: Hostname the MX record points at (e.g. mxext1.mailbox.org). Consult your mail provider's documentation.

parameters.name: Own mail domain (configure Cloudflare DNS)
parameters.description: When checked, create MX and SPF DNS records in Cloudflare for the mail domain (requires a cloudflare stack with CF_TOKEN). Leave unchecked if using an existing hosted mail setup where DNS already points to the provider.

parameters.name: SMTP Password
parameters.description: SMTP provider password. Provided via the oidc stack. If empty, mail DNS setup is skipped (no point in writing MX/SPF records when SMTP can't authenticate).

parameters.name: SMTP Port
parameters.description: SMTP server port (587 for STARTTLS, 465 for implicit TLS).

parameters.name: SMTP Sender
parameters.description: From address on outgoing mail (e.g. admin@example.com). Must be authorized at the provider.

parameters.name: SPF TXT value
parameters.description: Full SPF TXT record value, e.g. 'v=spf1 include:mailbox.org ~all'. Consult your mail provider's documentation.

parameters.name: SMTP User
parameters.description: SMTP authentication username (e.g. yourname@mailbox.org).

parameters.name: SSL Mode
parameters.description: SSL mode: proxy (nginx reverse proxy), native (app handles HTTPS), or certs (certificates only).

parameters.name: Needs CA Cert
parameters.description: Write CA public certificate (chain.pem)

parameters.name: Needs Server Cert
parameters.description: Write server certificate files (privkey.pem, cert.pem, fullchain.pem)

parameters.name: Stack ID
parameters.description: Auto-injected by backend: Primary stack ID for dependency discovery

parameters.name: Stack Secret Names
parameters.description: Comma-separated NAME=VALUE pairs for secrets to sanitize in template file

parameters.name: Startup Down Delay
parameters.description: Optional: Seconds to wait after shutting down this container before stopping the next one. Maps to Proxmox --startup down=N.

parameters.name: Startup Order
parameters.description: Optional: Boot order for the container. Lower values start first (e.g. 10 for databases, 20 for auth providers). Default 100 for normal apps. Maps to Proxmox --startup order=N.

parameters.name: Startup Timeout
parameters.description: Seconds to wait for Docker services to become healthy (--wait-timeout).

parameters.name: Startup Up Delay
parameters.description: Optional: Seconds to wait after starting this container before starting the next one. Maps to Proxmox --startup up=N.

parameters.name: static_gw
parameters.description: Static IPv4 gateway (e.g. 192.168.4.1). Requires static_ip.

parameters.name: static_gw6
parameters.description: Static IPv6 gateway (e.g. fd00::1). Requires static_ip6.

parameters.name: static_ip
parameters.description: Static IPv4 address in CIDR notation (e.g. 192.168.4.100/24).

parameters.name: static_ip6
parameters.description: Static IPv6 address in CIDR notation (e.g. fd00::50/64).

parameters.name: Storage
parameters.description: Proxmox storage where the OCI image should be stored

parameters.name: Storage Selection
parameters.description: Select storage to mount. Only filesystems prefixed with 'uuid:' are supported.

parameters.name: Target Versions
parameters.description: Comma-separated service=version pairs. For OCI-image apps: main=version to pull a specific tag.

parameters.name: Path to the LXC template
parameters.description: Path to the LXC template (e.g. local:vztmpl/alpine-3.19-default_20231107_amd64.tar.xz). Required for new installations (auto-selected for Alpine). Not needed in reconfiguration mode when vm_id points to an existing container.

parameters.name: Test Parameter
parameters.description: Test parameter for Python script

parameters.name: Test Value
parameters.description: Test value for Python script

parameters.name: UID
parameters.description: Optional: UID of the user in the container who should have access to the input device (LXC only). Default is 0 (root). For VMs, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: flows.json
parameters.description: Optional Node-RED flows file (flows.json). When omitted, Node-RED starts with empty flows.

parameters.name: Destination Path
parameters.description: Target path: {volume_key}:{filename}

parameters.name: modbus2mqtt local settings ZIP
parameters.description: ZIP file produced by 'Download Local Settings' (https://<host>:<port>/specifications). Contains busses, slaves and local specifications. secrets.yaml is intentionally excluded — set MQTT credentials via the UI after first boot.

parameters.name: Mosquitto Configuration
parameters.description:
Upload your mosquitto.conf file.

If the optional addon "SSL/HTTPS" is enabled, TLS certificates are automatically provisioned at:
- `/mosquitto/config/certs/cert.pem` (server cert)
- `/mosquitto/config/certs/privkey.pem` (private key)
- `/mosquitto/config/certs/chain.pem` (CA cert)
- `/mosquitto/config/certs/fullchain.pem` (server cert + CA)

MQTTS listener example:
```
listener 8883
certfile /mosquitto/config/certs/cert.pem
cafile /mosquitto/config/certs/chain.pem
keyfile /mosquitto/config/certs/privkey.pem
```
See [Mosquitto TLS docs](https://mosquitto.org/man/mosquitto-conf-5.html)

parameters.name: Destination Path
parameters.description: Target path: {volume_key}:{filename}

parameters.name: server.cert
parameters.description: Configuration file: server.cert

parameters.name: Destination Path
parameters.description: Target path: {volume_key}:{filename}

parameters.name: server.crt
parameters.description: Configuration file: server.crt

parameters.name: Destination Path
parameters.description: Target path: {volume_key}:{filename}

parameters.name: server.key
parameters.description: Configuration file: server.key

parameters.name: Destination Path
parameters.description: Target path: {volume_key}:{filename}

parameters.name: settings.js
parameters.description: Configuration file: settings.js

parameters.name: Destination Path
parameters.description: Target path: {volume_key}:{filename}

parameters.name: ssl.cnf
parameters.description: Configuration file: ssl.cnf

parameters.name: Destination Path
parameters.description: Target path: {volume_key}:{filename}

parameters.name: USB Bus:Device
parameters.description: USB bus and device number in format bus:device (e.g., 1:3 for /dev/bus/usb/001/003)

parameters.name: Username
parameters.description: Optional: Username to create on the VE host before container creation. This ensures consistent UID/GID mapping between host and container. Default is root. If not provided, root will be used.

parameters.name: VE Context Key
parameters.description: Auto-injected by backend: VE context identifier for API calls.

parameters.name: VE Debug Commands
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of command name substrings to debug-dump during execution. For each matching command the backend emits the current inputs+defaults (before) and outputs (after) to stderr of the deploy log. Use '*' to dump every command. Leave empty to disable. Example: 'Write Docker Compose Notes,acme-renew'

parameters.name: Version
parameters.description: Version of the package to install

parameters.name: LXC Container ID
parameters.description: VM ID — used to look up the running container's actual hostname (the one volumes are named after, which can differ from the input hostname during reconfigure).

parameters.name: VM ID Start
parameters.description: Optional: Start index for auto-assigned VM IDs. The next free ID starting from this value will be used.

parameters.name: Include in Backup
parameters.description: Whether the volume should be included in Proxmox backups

parameters.name: Volume Size
parameters.description: Default size per managed mp volume (e.g., 4M, 256M, 1G, 2.5G). Most volumes hold KB of state (config, secrets, certs, bootstrap PATs); the default fits those. Per-volume override is supported by appending `,size=…` to a line in the `volumes` property: e.g. `data=/var/lib/gitea,size=2G`.

parameters.name: Volume Storage
parameters.description: Proxmox storage ID for data volumes. Auto-detected from rootfs if not set.

parameters.name: Volumes
parameters.description: Existing volume definitions with options (e.g. permissions). Merged with extracted volumes.

parameters.name: Wait for Network
parameters.description: Wrap entrypoint to wait for network before starting. Only needed for apps that connect to external services at startup.

parameters.name: Create PostgREST Roles
parameters.description: Create additional API schema and roles for PostgREST (api_login, <app>_anon, <app>_user)

parameters.name: Zitadel Admin Password
parameters.description: Raw password value from the oidc stack (suffix is added at print time)

parameters.name: Zitadel External Domain
parameters.description: Public FQDN Zitadel is exposed under. When set, used as Host header and issuer-URL base instead of hostname+domain_suffix.

parameters.name: External Port
parameters.description: Public TCP port Zitadel is reached on (e.g. 443 behind nginx, 8080 for direct exposure).

parameters.name: External Secure
parameters.description: Set to 'true' when Zitadel is reached via HTTPS from outside (e.g. behind a TLS reverse proxy).

parameters.name: Forwarded Proto
parameters.description: X-Forwarded-Proto value the login UI sends to zitadel-api (http or https). Match the public scheme.

parameters.name: Zitadel Host
parameters.description: Hostname of the Zitadel instance (resolved via dependency)

parameters.name: Zitadel PAT
parameters.description: Short-lived Zitadel access token (injected by backend in zero-secret mode). Falls back to admin-client.pat file if not set.

parameters.name: Zitadel Project ID
parameters.description: Output from bootstrap step (presence triggers hardening)

parameters.name: Public Base URL
parameters.description: Full public base URL the user types in their browser (no trailing slash). Used in BASEURI/OIDC default URLs. Example: https://auth.example.com (no port for default 443/80).
